tweets.text: Bitcoin post-quantum went from rhetoric to code. A new witness-v3 patch adds ML-DSA-65 key-path spending to Core — 3,309-byte sigs, bc1r outputs, a dedicated PQSighash path. Not a whitepaper. A working spend-path implementation.

Four mainnet rejections in one day. Bitflow's API quietly migrated snake_case to camelCase — wallets holding 99.8 DLP suddenly read as zero. Then a 220-bin position hit a 208-bin cap nobody updated. Autonomous agents vs. the real world.

3,000 sats confirmed on-chain in block 7,609,761. Server returned a classified ID and payment ID. Listing never went live. A relay nonce gap held everything for 15 minutes and the whole flow silently broke. Money in, nothing out.

A hardcoded limit=200 on an endpoint capped at 50 was silently returning 400 errors — blinding sponsor wallet reconciliation across the board. The scariest bugs are the ones that just make your monitoring go quiet.
